name: ledger-dev-tools
description: Tools to accelerate development of apps for Ledger devices.

commands.title: Select device
commands.tooltip: Select the device you want to build your app for.

commands.title: Select variant
commands.tooltip: Select the app variant you want to build.

commands.title: Select build use case
commands.tooltip: Select the use case you want to build with.

commands.title: Select test use case
commands.tooltip: Select the test use case you want to run, when manifest contains multiple test use cases.

commands.title: Select app
commands.tooltip: Select the app you want to build.

commands.title: Add test dependencies
commands.tooltip: Add additional test dependencies to install for selected app.

commands.title: Select Guideline Enforcer Check
commands.tooltip: Select the check you want to run, or All by default.

commands.title: AI Review
commands.tooltip: Analyze modified files against Ledger embedded application rules using AI.

ledgerDevTools.dockerRunArgs.description: Additional arguments to pass to the 'docker run' command for the Ledger developer tools Docker image.

ledgerDevTools.onboardingSeed.default: glory promote mansion idle axis finger extra february uncover one trip resource lawn turtle enact monster seven myth punch hobby comfort wild raise skin
ledgerDevTools.onboardingSeed.markdownDescription: Device quick onboarding default 24-word Seed phrase.

ledgerDevTools.additionalReqsPerApp.description: Additional functional tests prerequisites to install in the current app's docker container.

ledgerDevTools.keepTerminal.markdownDescription: Keep the Terminal window opened after a successful task execution.

ledgerDevTools.openContainerAsRoot.markdownDescription: Open docker container using root user.

ledgerDevTools.containerUpdateRetries.markdownDescription: Max number of Container Update retries.

ledgerDevTools.userScpPrivateKey.markdownDescription: Use the host's `SCP_PRIVKEY` environment variable when loading/deleting app on device. Cf. https://developers.ledger.com/docs/embedded-app/pin-bypass.

ledgerDevTools.enableDeviceOpsForNanoX.markdownDescription: Allow device operations on Nano X (requires special development device)

ledgerDevTools.dockerAutoUpdate.markdownDescription: Automatically update the Ledger developer tools Docker image in the background whenever the development container is started or is not ready.

walkthroughs.title: Welcome to Ledger Developer Tools
walkthroughs.description: Get started with building Ledger device apps. Check the prerequisites, then follow the path that matches your use case.

steps.title: Check Prerequisites
steps.description: Ensure Docker, Git, and an X11 server are installed and running before using the extension.

steps.title: Create your Ledger app
steps.description:
Choose this path to build a new device app in C or Rust.
Generate your app from a template and start customizing it.

steps.title: Create a Bitcoin clone app
steps.description: Choose this path to fork the Bitcoin extension boilerplate and support a Bitcoin-derived chain on Ledger devices.

steps.title: Add a custom EVM chain
steps.description: Choose this path to register a custom EVM network so the Ethereum signer app can display it on device when signing transactions.

steps.title: Clear sign your EVM smart contract transactions
steps.description: Choose this path to make your smart contract transactions human-readable on Ledger devices using the ERC-7730 standard.

steps.title: Learn more
steps.description: Explore the Ledger Developer Portal to learn about app development, requirements, and the submission process.

walkthroughs.title: Customize your C Device App
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to customize your C device app created with the Ledger Developer Tools extension.

steps.title: Set your app name
steps.description: Choose a name for your Ledger device app.

steps.title: Design your app icon
steps.description: Provide device-specific icons for your Ledger app.

steps.title: Configure app flags
steps.description: Define specific flags for your app to enable or disable certain features during the build process.

steps.title: Set derivation path & curves
steps.description: Specify the derivation path and cryptographic curves your app will support.

steps.title: Choose app variants
steps.description: Select different variants of your app to cater to various use cases.

steps.title: Register your app in the database
steps.description: Add your app to the Ledger app database to pass the mandatory compliance check.

walkthroughs.title: Customize your Rust Device App
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to customize your Rust device app created with the Ledger Developer Tools extension.

steps.title: Set your app name
steps.description: Define your app name in Cargo.toml and in the UI source code.

steps.title: Design your app icon
steps.description: Provide device-specific icons and glyphs for your Ledger app.

steps.title: Set install parameters
steps.description: Configure path, curve, and flags from [package.metadata.ledger] in Cargo.toml.

steps.title: Register your app in the database
steps.description: Add your app to the Ledger app database to pass the mandatory compliance check.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
